published.text:
### Hero
Heading: CRM Automation Setup Cost for Contractors
Body: If you are pricing contractor CRM setup, the useful question is not just “what does a contractor CRM expert charge?” It is “how much workflow mess am I actually trying to fix?” A contractor with one owner, a light lead volume, and a simple pipeline should not pay for the same build as a team that needs estimate follow-up, dispatch handoff, office-to-field context, reporting discipline, and cleaner integration with field-service software. This page keeps the decision narrow: realistic contractor CRM setup pricing, what pushes the cost up, when a smaller build is enough, and how to tell whether expert setup is cheaper than another month of sloppy handoff and lost jobs.
Sub: Below: what different contractor CRM setup scopes usually cost, what makes a build expensive, when DIY or a lighter workflow is still enough, and how to sanity-check the payback before you overbuild.

### Core sections
- Frames the page as the pricing decision inside the existing contractor CRM cluster rather than a rewrite of the broader setup-help guide.
- Breaks down realistic setup ranges for intake cleanup, estimate follow-up, dispatch handoff, messy-account rebuilds, and hybrid CRM-plus-field-service stacks.
- Explains what actually drives cost up for contractors: office-to-field handoff complexity, integration depth, dirty data, and trust-risk around live workflows.
- Keeps the proof framing honest by using the live contractor cluster plus the published CRM case study as adjacent proof instead of inventing a contractor-specific pricing case study.

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers for contractors budgeting CRM implementation work
Topics: focused build cost ranges, how this differs from the general contractor setup page, what makes the project expensive, Jobber/Housecall Pro/ServiceTitan fit, and how to judge whether the setup price is worth it.

### Final CTA
Heading: Want a realistic price range for your contractor CRM setup?
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will look at your current intake, estimate, scheduling, and dispatch flow, then give you a practical scope and price range for the narrowest CRM build worth doing first.

edits.description: Shipped because the exact pricing query “How much does contractor CRM setup cost?” was still buried inside the live `/crm-automation-setup-for-contractors` tracked prompts, making this a distinct, high-intent child page with direct commercial relevance.
Kept the copy practical and non-hype by centering scoping, handoff complexity, integration depth, and cleanup risk instead of generic automation promises.
Treated the contractor workflow cluster and the published CRM case study as honest adjacent proof while explicitly avoiding any invented contractor-specific setup metrics.
